mnemonics: F.S.P.S.C. - Forking, Signals, Pthreads, System Calls (Four key threading issues to remember)
Think 'T.H.R.E.A.D.' - Thread-local storage, Handling signals, Race conditions, Efficiency, Asynchronous cancels, Daemon status
keyPoints: The fork() system call behavior differs between processes with threads: the entire process is duplicated, but only the calling thread is copied in the new process
Signal handling is complex in multithreaded programs because a signal can be delivered to any thread, requiring careful design to avoid unpredictable behavior
Thread cancellation must be handled carefully to avoid resource leaks and inconsistent states, using either deferred or asynchronous cancellation methods
commonMistakes: Assuming fork() duplicates all threads in a process (it only duplicates the calling thread)
Believing that signals are delivered to the entire process rather than to a specific thread
Using asynchronous thread cancellation without proper cleanup handlers, leading to resource leaks and inconsistent states
